name: Vindaloo
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind the cumin seeds, red chilies, peppercorns, cardamom seeds, cinnamon sticks, black mustard seeds, and fenugreek seeds in a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
2. Combine the ground spices with vinegar, salt, and brown sugar in a bowl. Mix well and set aside.
3. Heat oil in a large, heavy pot over medium heat. Add onions and fry, stirring frequently, until they are golden and crisp. Remove the onions with a slotted spoon and transfer to a blender or food processor. Turn off the heat. Add 2–3 tablespoons water and blend until smooth. Return the onion purée to the spice mixture.
4. Drain pork cubes on paper towels and discard any large fat pieces.
5. Blend ginger, garlic, water, and set aside.
6. Heat remaining oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown pork cubes in batches, removing them with a slotted spoon each time. Return meat to the pot. Add the ginger-garlic paste, then coriander and turmeric. Stir for a few seconds.
7. Add pork, juices, vindaloo paste, and water. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer gently until pork is tender (about 1 hour). Stir occasionally during cooking.
8. Serve with rice.
